mechanism_narrative: Sustained near-zero federal funds rates reflect an accommodative monetary policy stance that reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% do not exhibit significantly positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income growth and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or walk-forward Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declining retail sales and rising unemployment jointly indicate a consumer-led economic contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month decline concurrent with UNRATE 3-month increase
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of falling RSAFS and rising UNRATE do not produce negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or interaction term t-statistic falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during post-crisis recovery periods signal a durable rebound in residential investment and construction employment, which drives broad-based equity appreciation through demand multiplier effects over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with elevated unemployment and near-zero policy rates
failure_condition: Positive 3-month changes in HOUST during post-crisis recovery regimes do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding one percent over three months signals manufacturing sector expansion and rising capacity utilization, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO 3-month change exceeds 1.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in quarterly real GDP growth signals expanding aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly increases in GDPC1 fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns (walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rising federal funds rates and elevated CPI inflation jointly increase real borrowing costs and compress profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month increase > 0.25pp while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > 3%
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL inflation above 3% do not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 400 thousand persons over three months signals robust labor demand and wage-driven consumption expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS 3-month gains exceeding 400k are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered observations.
